reason: Creek flowing under culvert. Abundant juveniles upstream and downstream. Determine next steps (priority) with WG
notes: Likely would be partially passable if creek wasn't flowing beneath culvert
pscis_assessment_comment: Culvert appeared to have shifted away from the banks, with the creek flowing beneath it. Debris was built up around the culvert inlet, and the outlet was embedded with sediment. Many juvenile fish were observed both upstream and downstream, including in the pool at culvert outlet. The habitat confirmation identified no barriers in the 300 m reviewed upstream and 300 m reviewed downstream.  Recommend replacing with an open bottom structure greater than 2 m wide to ensure it does not get fully plugged, to provide fish passage for all life-stages at all flows to the extensive length of high valuable habitat upstream.

reason: High quality habitat, fish present upstream and downstream

reason: Dry channel. Recommend excluding due to lack of habitat
pscis_assessment_comment: Habitat Wizard identified WCT and EB 600 m downstream, and no barriers present. The channel was dry at the time of assessment. A detailed habitat assessment was not conducted due to private property constraints. Given the ephemeral nature of the channel and low habitat gain potential, further assessment and improvements is considered a low priority. If further assessment was to be conducted, permission from the landowner would first be required. The applicable improvement could be to replace the two existing 0.6 m diameter culverts with one suitably sized culvert (1 m diameter minimum).

reason: Upstream habitat confirmation was not completed due to private land. Complete habitat confirmation upstream
notes: Fish present downstream. Habitat confirmation recommended.
pscis_assessment_comment: Fish were historically sampled upstream (BT, WCT, and EB in 1990; Province of BC 2024). The assessment identified high value habitat immediately upstream of the crossing, with fish observed 250 m downstream. There were no barriers evident in the 300 m reviewed downstream of the culvert. The gradient upstream of the culvert remained low (2%). There was a debris jam within the culvert. It was private land upstream, and with landowner’s consent, a habitat confirmation  with fish sampling is recommended to verify value and available upstream habitat length. The likely crossing fix would be to replace with an open bottom structure, or a streambed simulation closed bottom culvert of approx. 2.5 m diameter. This would ensure passage for all life-stages at all flows to the extensive length of stream available upstream.

reason: Medium quality habitat with abundant WCT fry observed. Adult passage likely hindered by beaver grate. Recommend advancing to priority barriers list

Increasing the beaver debris blockage mesh size is also recommended to allow adult fish to pass upstream.

reason: High quality habitat. Crossing heavily impacted by beaver activity. Recommend advancing to priority barrier but need to determine EB risk first.
notes: North Coal may have potential plans for this crossing. Need to determine the Brook Trout situation in the wetland before committing to any rehabilitation of this structure.
pscis_assessment_comment: PSCIS assessment identified this crossing to only be a potential barrier. EB were evident immediately upstream and downstream of crossing. The host of fish species were also known 100 m downstream in Michel Creek. Upstream habitat was significantly impacted by beaver activity (Crossing 50255 was within 30 m upstream). An open bottom structure, or a larger diameter culvert would help preclude damming and improve fish movement and floodplain processes.

reason: High quality habitat with WCT upstream. EB downstream. Beaver grates and debris buildup causing inlet drops. Recommend advancing as priority barrier but need to determine EB risk first.
notes: North Coal may have potential plans for this crossing. Need to determine the Brook Trout situation in the wetland before committing to any rehabilitation of this structure.
pscis_assessment_comment: Stream impacted by beaver activity. The crossing was comprised of three culverts, side by side, all with beaver grates at inlets and outlets. The culverts required maintenance due to accumulation of woody debris and associated inlet drops. A host of fish species were known to be present downstream in Michel Creek, and WCT and EB were previously sampled upstream in the tributary (in 2014 and 2015; Province of BC 2024). There were no barriers evident in the 300 m reviewed upstream and 30 m downstream (site 50254) of the culvert. The gradient upstream of the culvert was low at 2%. An open bottom structure, or a larger diameter culvert would help preclude damming and improve fish movement and floodplain processes.

reason: Small stream with mostly fine substrates. WCT presence this high in system unknown. 371 m modeled rearing habitat upstream. Recommend non-actionable
notes: Culvert likely at least partially passable with some maintenance to remove debris causing inlet drop.
pscis_assessment_comment: Habitat value medium based on small channel with low flow, absence of gravels, and high overhanging vegetation. Culvert was a barrier. The habitat confirmation assessment only went 150 m downstream and 300 m upstream, with no fish sampling completed due to very shallow stream depths and lack of remaining field time. Although Habitat Wizard only showed the nearest fish point to be 2.64 km downstream (WCT near confluence with the Fording R), it is generally known that fish are present in Grace Creek downstream of the culvert with no barrier present downstream to the Fording River. Replace with an open bottom crossing or a closed bottom structure with streambed simulation

reason: Important WCT stream with high-quality habitat upstream
notes: Barrier owner non-responsive. Currently being proposed as offsetting site for NWP Coal. Awaiting DFO decision.
pscis_assessment_comment: Excellent habitat with abundant cover and deep pools present for rearing and overwintering. Abundant spawning gravel present. WCT captured with electrofishing downstream of culvert and WCT also observed upstream. Good candidate for replacement. Crossing 62418 (PSCIS ID) should be done concomitantly.
